name: Chestnut-And-Lentil Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Carrot (diced into small, even squares)
1 large Garlic clove (peeled and finely chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Score chestnuts on their rounded sides and toss them in just enough oil to lightly coat them.
3. Place chestnuts on a baking sheet. Bake until skins have opened and meat is tender, about 20 minutes.
4. When cool enough to handle, peel and dice the chestnuts into small pieces. Set aside.
5. Rinse lentils and combine them in a soup pot with water. Bring to boil.
6. Cook a few minutes at gentle boil, removing foam that forms on surface.
7. Add carrot, celery, onion, garlic, bay leaf, salt, and parsley. Lower heat and simmer until lentils are tender, about 35 minutes.
8. When done, remove parsley sprigs and bay leaf. Correct seasoning and add salt if necessary.
9. Puree half the lentils until smooth, then recombine them with the rest.
Finishing the Soup:
1. In a large pan, warm olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add roasted chestnuts and herbs. Cook, stirring frequently, for several minutes.
3. Add wine and tomato paste. Stir to dissolve tomato paste and cook a few minutes to reduce wine.
4. Stir in lentil mixture and simmer gently for 20 minutes. If soup is too thick, thin it with a little water, stock, or cream until the right consistency.
5. Taste and season with pepper.
6. Serve with a spoonful of olive oil swirled into each bowl. Sprinkle with parsley and croutons if desired.
